sensitivity: 84.5% [74.6%, 94.2%] for convulsive seizures; 84.3% [75.2%, 91.9%] for other major motor seizures
intended_use: Nelli is indicated for use as an adjunct to seizure monitoring of patients aged 18 years and above in healthcare facilities during periods of rest. The device utilizes automated analysis of audio and
intended_use_full: Nelli is indicated for use as an adjunct to seizure monitoring of patients aged 18 years and above in healthcare facilities during periods of rest. The device utilizes automated analysis of audio and video (media) to identify epileptic and non-epileptic seizure events with a positive motor component. The system provides prioritization of identified major seizures and other motor events for review and media playback by a qualified healthcare professional.
target_condition: Convulsive seizures (tonic-clonic and bilateral clonic) and other major motor seizures (tonic, unilateral clonic, hyperkinetic seizures)
ground_truth: Expert panel review of video EEG (described as the current gold standard for seizure monitoring)
other_metrics: False Detection Rate (FDR) for convulsive seizures: 0.050 FP/hour [0.0376, 0.0636] or 1.20 FP/24 hours [0.902, 1.53]; FDR for other major motor seizures: 6.08 FP/hour [5.63, 6.54] or 146 FP/24 hours [135, 157]
